evals.prompt: Create a new design direction for our React pricing page. First interview me, inspect the current pages and tech, then write DESIGN.md before planning implementation.
evals.expected_output: Runs an interview/inspection phase, identifies framework and styling context, creates or updates DESIGN.md before planning, and routes the artifact through design-reviewer.
assertions: The workflow starts with interview and repository inspection before visual decisions
DESIGN.md is created or updated before implementation planning

evals.prompt: Use our DESIGN.md to plan a dashboard redesign with filters, queue state, selected item detail, empty/error/loading states, and mobile behavior.
evals.expected_output: Plans from DESIGN.md, classifies the surface as product UI, covers states and responsive behavior, and sends the plan to design-reviewer before implementation.
assertions: The plan is explicitly grounded in DESIGN.md
Operational states and reviewer gating are included before implementation

evals.prompt: Implement the approved plan for the landing page and keep iterating until the design review passes.
evals.expected_output: Implements in the existing frontend stack, verifies rendered desktop/mobile behavior when possible, runs the scanner where relevant, and loops through design-reviewer until pass or a real blocker is named.
assertions: Implementation follows an approved plan and existing stack conventions
Final completion depends on rendered checks and a design-reviewer verdict

evals.prompt: Rework this homepage because the last version looked terrible. Do not stop until the design review is actually satisfied.
evals.expected_output: Audits the rendered rework against the original ask and DESIGN.md, sends screenshots/scanner/build evidence to design-reviewer, fixes any FAIL or PASS WITH FIXES findings, and only closes on PASS or explicit user acceptance of named remaining defects.
assertions: The implementation loop treats review as a blocking gate rather than optional advice
PASS WITH FIXES is not treated as final sign-off for a user-facing visual rework

evals.prompt: Audit the current app, examples, colors, typography, and component tech, then create a DESIGN.md that future agents can follow.
evals.expected_output: Inspects current UI, source files, tokens, colors, typography, components, and technology before writing a compact DESIGN.md with source-of-truth notes.
assertions: Current colors, typography, components, and technology are inspected
The resulting DESIGN.md contains reusable rules and source-of-truth references

evals.prompt: This shadcn/Tailwind homepage looks AI-generated. Build a less generic version using a real visual direction, not just another default palette.
evals.expected_output: Uses interview or explicit assumptions to choose a product-specific direction, documents it in DESIGN.md, avoids common AI tells, and verifies with scanner plus rendered review when possible.
assertions: The replacement direction is specific to the product rather than another generic default
Common AI-looking visual tells are checked with both scanner and rendered review

evals.prompt: Design both the public homepage and the internal billing operations screen. They should not use the same visual rules.
evals.expected_output: Classifies homepage as brand and billing operations as product, records route-level differences in DESIGN.md, and plans distinct layout, density, copy, and motion rules.
assertions: Brand and product registers are distinguished before styling
DESIGN.md captures route-level differences rather than forcing one look everywhere

evals.prompt: Fix the retry logic in our API client and add a regression test.
evals.expected_output: Should not require design-engineer because this is non-visual frontend or backend implementation.

evals.prompt: Review this completed homepage screenshot and tell me whether the design passes or fails.
evals.expected_output: Should prefer design-reviewer because the user asks for standalone critique and pass/fail review, not creation or implementation.
